joke.raw_joke: My daughter finally started looking for a job. I told her there's an entry level job that people are dying to get into and you start with 3000 people beneath you. — Working at the graveyard isn't for everyone though!

blocks.content:
RILEY_CASTING_PLAN_v1

Primary Cast (2-3 roles):
1. role_needed: Dad narrator / setup
   selected_cast_persona: jerry · suburban-detective
   why_this_fit: Deadpan delivery sells the graveyard misdirect.
2. role_needed: Daughter job-seeker
   selected_cast_persona: zoe · optimistic-teacher
   why_this_fit: Friendly, readable young-adult energy for daughter role.
3. role_needed: Background cemetery worker (optional)
   selected_cast_persona: vince · handyman
   why_this_fit: Adds setting cue without stealing focus.

Adaptation Notes:
1. Use neutral cemetery setting props (clipboard, headstones).
2. Keep tone playful, not dark-horror.

Shot Intent:
- setup_shot: Setup: dad + daughter talking about jobs (medium two-shot).
- punchline_reaction_shot: Punchline: dad reaction close-up with graveyard reveal background.

---

IRIS_STORYBOARD_v1
seed_id: seed-001
aspect_ratio: 9:16 (portrait)
canvas_target: 1080x1920
duration_target_sec: 9.4
objective: Build a low-cost visual blueprint before paid video generation.

storyboard_frames:
1) frame_id: S1_hook
   time_range: 0.0–1.8s
   shot_type: Medium two-shot
   composition: Jerry left third, Zoe right third, top safe margin for captions
   action: Daughter asks about jobs; both facing slightly toward camera
   prompt_still: "9:16 cartoon frame, dad and daughter talking near cemetery entrance gate, bright playful palette, clean 2.5D linework, expressive faces, family-safe, no text"

2) frame_id: S2_setup
   time_range: 1.8–4.6s
   shot_type: Medium close-up on Jerry
   composition: Jerry centered with shallow cemetery cue in background
   action: Jerry delivers setup with deadpan confidence; eyebrow raise
   prompt_still: "9:16 medium close-up cartoon dad suburban detective look, delivering joke setup, soft cemetery hints in background, bright colors, clean outlines, family-safe, no text"

3) frame_id: S3_reveal
   time_range: 4.6–7.6s
   shot_type: Context reveal shot
   composition: Wider frame showing gate/headstones in soft focus; Zoe puzzled foreground reaction
   action: Visual reveal of cemetery context to support misdirect
   prompt_still: "9:16 cartoon reveal shot, daughter puzzled in foreground, cemetery gate and a few stylized headstones softly visible behind, playful not scary, high readability, no text"

4) frame_id: S4_punchline
   time_range: 7.6–9.4s
   shot_type: Tight close-up on Jerry
   composition: Face-centered, lower-third safe for punchline caption, minimal background clutter
   action: Final deadpan line + 0.4s hold for reaction
   prompt_still: "9:16 tight close-up cartoon dad, deadpan expression after joke punchline, clean background separation, bright family-safe style, no text"

caption_safe_guide:
- top_safe: 12% height
- bottom_safe: 18% height
- avoid placing key face features in bottom 18%

handoff_for_generation:
- generate 1 still per frame_id first (4 stills total)
- if approved by Jerry: allow 2 alternates only for weakest frame
- do NOT start paid full-video generation until storyboard approval

status: storyboard_ready_for_jerry_review

---

IRIS_VIDEO_PROMPT_v2

seed_id: seed-001
aspect_ratio: 9:16 (vertical portrait)
concept_count: 3

Concept A — Performance-First Character Comedy
duration_target: 9.4s
duration_reason: Two-character setup plus reveal beat needs a clean pause before punchline. Keep straightforward continuity cuts for readability.
master_video_prompt:
Family-safe cartoon short, bright suburban cemetery edge at golden hour, cast jerry suburban-detective + zoe optimistic-teacher (+ optional vince handyman distant extra), expressive reaction faces, readable props, clean outlines, punchline-driven pacing, 9:16 composition with top/bottom caption-safe bands, medium-to-close framing, smooth push-in on punchline. Prioritize facial acting and clean two-shot/close-up rhythm; minimal background motion.
scene_plan:
1) Scene 1 (0.0–1.8s): Hook — medium two-shot, daughter asks about jobs, slight handheld sway, upbeat expression.
2) Scene 2 (1.8–4.6s): Setup — dad explains “entry level… 3000 people beneath you,” subtle eyebrow raise, slow push-in.
3) Scene 3 (4.6–7.6s): Visual turn — reveal cemetery background signs/headstones in soft focus, daughter puzzled reaction.
4) Scene 4 (7.6–9.4s): Punchline payoff — dad deadpan close-up: “graveyard isn’t for everyone,” hold 0.4s reaction.
caption_plan:
1. 0.1s: “My daughter finally started looking for a job…”
2. 2.2s: “There’s an entry level job people are DYING to get into…”
3. 4.2s: “You start with 3000 people beneath you.”
4. 7.8s: “Working at the graveyard isn’t for everyone though!”
performance_notes:
- Friendly setup pace, 0.35–0.45s pause before final line, deliver punchline dry. Balanced pace, clear pause before punchline.
risk_and_fallback:
1. Risk: cemetery could read too dark → keep palette bright/comedic and no spooky horror assets.
2. Fallback: if background clutter fails, use single tombstone icon and reaction close-up.
why_jerry_might_greenlight_this:
- Most reliable laugh-read and easiest to produce under budget/time pressure.
vince_handoff:
- aspectRatio: 9:16
- durationSec: 9.4
- sceneCount: 4
- mustKeep: Scene 2 setup line + Scene 4 punchline close-up
- optionalTrims: Trim Scene 3 from 3.0s to 1.8s if needed

---

Concept B — Kinetic Camera + Visual Gag Emphasis
duration_target: 8.8s
duration_reason: Two-character setup plus reveal beat needs a clean pause before punchline. Add one energetic transition beat between setup and punchline.
master_video_prompt:
Family-safe cartoon short, bright suburban cemetery edge at golden hour, cast jerry suburban-detective + zoe optimistic-teacher (+ optional vince handyman distant extra), expressive reaction faces, readable props, clean outlines, punchline-driven pacing, 9:16 composition with top/bottom caption-safe bands, medium-to-close framing, smooth push-in on punchline. Use dynamic push-ins, whip pans, and one stronger visual gag transition while preserving caption readability.

3. 4.2s: “You start with 3000 people beneath you.”
4. 7.8s: “Working at the graveyard isn’t for everyone though!”
performance_notes:

risk_and_fallback:
1. Risk: cemetery could read too dark → keep palette bright/comedic and no spooky horror assets.
2. Fallback: if background clutter fails, use single tombstone icon and reaction close-up.
why_jerry_might_greenlight_this:

duration_target: 10.2s
duration_reason: Two-character setup plus reveal beat needs a clean pause before punchline. Include one contextual insert shot (prop/sign/location) to strengthen premise clarity.

Family-safe cartoon short, bright suburban cemetery edge at golden hour, cast jerry suburban-detective + zoe optimistic-teacher (+ optional vince handyman distant extra), expressive reaction faces, readable props, clean outlines, punchline-driven pacing, 9:16 composition with top/bottom caption-safe bands, medium-to-close framing, smooth push-in on punchline. Lean into environment storytelling and one extra reaction/cutaway to sell context before payoff.

3) Scene 3 (4.6–7.6s): Visual turn — reveal cemetery background signs/headstones in soft focus, daughter puzzled reaction.

2. Fallback: if background clutter fails, use single tombstone icon and reaction close-up.

JERRY_GREENLIGHT_v1
selected_concept: A
decision: GREENLIGHT
confidence: High
rationale: Concept A has the cleanest setup-to-reveal path and strongest deadpan payoff with low failure modes. Cemetery context reads quickly without needing complex motion. Best budget-safe laugh conversion.
cost_note: Low complexity: 2 principals, simple location cues, minimal dynamic camera.
must_keep: Scene 2 setup line + Scene 4 punchline close-up

JERRY_MONEY_BRIEF_v1
seed_id: seed-001
selected_concept: A
objective: Deliver a clean, family-safe 9:16 short where the graveyard misdirect lands clearly and quickly.
format:
  - aspect_ratio: 9:16
  - target_duration_sec: 9.4
  - fps: 30
  - resolution_target: 1080x1920
cast_and_personas:
  - jerry · suburban-detective (dad narrator)
  - zoe · optimistic-teacher (daughter)
  - optional: vince · handyman (background cemetery worker, distant only)
final_script_lines:
  1) "My daughter finally started looking for a job."
  2) "I told her there's an entry level job that people are dying to get into..."
  3) "...and you start with 3000 people beneath you."
  4) "Working at the graveyard isn't for everyone though!"
scene_timeline:
  - Scene 1 (0.0–1.8s): Medium two-shot, daughter asks about jobs, upbeat tone.
  - Scene 2 (1.8–4.6s): Dad setup lines, slow push-in, eyebrows/reaction emphasis.
  - Scene 3 (4.6–7.6s): Reveal cemetery context in background (soft focus), daughter puzzled look.
  - Scene 4 (7.6–9.4s): Dad close-up punchline and reaction hold (~0.4s).
camera_and_motion_notes:
  - Keep camera grammar simple: medium -> push-in -> reveal -> close-up.
  - Avoid fast kinetic transitions for this one; prioritize clarity over flair.
caption_plan:
  - 0.1s: "My daughter finally started looking for a job..."
  - 2.2s: "There's an entry level job people are DYING to get into..."
  - 4.2s: "You start with 3000 people beneath you."
  - 7.8s: "Working at the graveyard isn't for everyone though!"
audio_voice_notes:
  - Conversational delivery; one clear pause before punchline.
  - Dad voice deadpan on final line; no spooky/horror performance.
must_keep:
  - Scene 2 setup cadence
  - Scene 4 punchline close-up + 0.4s reaction hold
optional_trims:
  - First trim: shorten Scene 3 reveal by up to 1.0s if runtime/complexity drifts.
cost_guardrails:
  - max_generation_attempts: 1
  - max_fallback_attempts: 1
  - stop_conditions: Stop if punchline clarity fails twice or caption timing breaks twice; escalate back to Jerry.
deliverable_checklist:
  - 9:16 export (1080x1920)
  - Runtime between 8.6s and 10.2s
  - Captions readable on mobile
  - Punchline lands clearly without extra explanation
handoff_to: Vince
